hardness_axes.name: Discover
hardness_axes.reasoning: The instruction states the symptom (the flattened tree disagrees with a kernel overlay mount on some stacks) but not the cause; the solver must read the pipeline and run it to discover that opaque masking is applied too broadly, a directory whiteout orphans its subtree, attribute resolution favours the wrong layer, and link groups are dereferenced.

hardness_axes.name: Synthesize
hardness_axes.reasoning: Correct output requires four interacting stages of a single pipeline to agree on overlay semantics; no one file holds the answer because the surviving-set, the attribute winner, and the link grouping are decided in different stages that consume each other's state.

hardness_axes.name: Diagnose or Design/Search
hardness_axes.reasoning: Repair shape: the instruction is symptoms-only, so the burden is diagnosing which of the four coordinated stages violates overlay semantics for a given class of stack, then repairing the logic without breaking the cases that already work.

hardness_axes.name: Navigate coupling
hardness_axes.reasoning: Local choices break distant invariants: making opaque sticky also drops upper additions; deleting only an exact whitened path leaves orphaned descendants a later stage emits; choosing the wrong attribute layer corrupts directories that downstream materialization writes; and a stale link group references an entry a higher layer removed.

hardness_axes.name: Reason beyond training
hardness_axes.reasoning: OCI whiteout conventions and kernel overlay union semantics (opaque masks only strictly-lower layers, copy-up takes the topmost contributor, tar link groups become shared inodes) are subsystem-specific rules that are routinely implemented incorrectly and are not a single textbook recipe.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Disclosure-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Even after the overlay rules are stated honestly in the docs and instruction, the residual work is locating the four diverging stages in a working pipeline and repairing coupled logic; full disclosure of the contract does not reveal the defects.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hidden-instance
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Difficulty is not about which one bundled stack is broken; the verifier flattens freshly generated stacks, so the fix must generalize to overlay semantics rather than a known instance.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Single-artifact repair
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: There is no single manifest or flag to flip; four disjoint defects in four stages must all be corrected, and any one left wrong fails the parity tests.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Generalization
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Seeded verify-time stacks defeat a solver that special-cases the bundled fixtures; the corrected semantics must hold on unseen layer combinations.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Prompt-honesty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: An honest prompt can describe the observable divergences and point at the public overlay contract without revealing where the pipeline goes wrong, because the symptoms do not localize to a stage.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Cheating-vs-difficulty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Verify-time stack generation and an output-tree walk are anti-cheating measures; the legitimate difficulty is the diagnosis and the coupled repair, not the anti-cheating wrapper.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Mechanical-fix filter
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The task is not about verifier dependencies, timeouts, or reward formatting; it is about the union-merge logic the solver must repair.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Localized-fix
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The fix spans four stage packages and the instruction names symptoms, not files or functions, so it does not collapse to one obvious site.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Oracle-locality
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The oracle rewrites four substantive stage helpers across four packages, not one whole-file overwrite, and adds real merge logic rather than reverting flags.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Small declarative-cluster
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The solve is algorithmic union-merge logic over an accumulated index, not a config block, a literal table, or a single helper overwrite.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Grep-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Instruction nouns (whiteout, opaque, ownership, hardlink) do not grep to the opaque stage helpers; the one package whose name matches a noun is the correct marker-detection package, an active decoy.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Pre-factored-helper
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Helper packages render diagnostics and aggregate counters and do not mirror the four stage concerns; the stage helpers carry opaque names that do not encode the contract.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Recipe-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Coordinated opaque/whiteout/copy-up/hardlink reconstruction across a multi-stage pipeline is not a standard safe-extraction or traversal recipe.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Security-aura discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: There is no security aura standing in for hardness; the residual reasoning is filesystem union semantics.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Orthogonal-checklist
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The four stages are coupled: opaque scope interacts with upper additions, subtree removal interacts with link-group membership, and copy-up interacts with re-created directories, so the fixes trade against one another rather than being independent.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Harness-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The Docker image, tar fixtures, and shell runners are realism; the hardness is the union-merge repair, not the harness.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: One-pass solvability
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: A frontier model cannot one-shot the four coordinated repairs after opening files; it must run the pipeline, observe divergences on several stack classes, and reason about which stage and which coupling each symptom implies.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hard-only gate
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: This is clearly hard, not merely respectable: a partial fix that handles the obvious whiteout case still fails opaque scope, copy-up direction, and link preservation, and the parity tests require all four at once.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Discovery/design budget test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: At least four non-trivial discoveries (opaque masks only strictly-lower layers, a directory whiteout removes the whole lower subtree and is confined below its origin, copy-up takes the topmost contributor including for directories, and link groups must survive as shared inodes minus removed members) are forced from the code, runtime, and docs rather than stated as a recipe.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Instruction specificity test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Instruction is symptoms-only for a repair shape: it names observable divergences and the public overlay contract but withholds the cause, the stage locations, and the merge algorithm.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Topology distribution test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Three repair topologies each require coordinating at least three locations (opaque scope plus the accumulated index plus emission; subtree removal plus link grouping plus emission; copy-up plus re-creation plus the shared types), and no single location passes the parity tests alone.

rubric_axes.name: Verifiable
rubric_axes.reasoning: The verifier builds layer stacks with known ground truth by construction, runs the solver, and walks the output tree to check presence, ownership, permissions, and shared inodes deterministically; seeded generation makes runs reproducible.

rubric_axes.name: Well-specified
rubric_axes.reasoning: Two engineers reading the instruction and the overlay-semantics doc would build equivalent verifiers from the stated contract: match a kernel overlay mount of the same layers, preserve link groups, and take topmost metadata.

rubric_axes.name: Solvable
rubric_axes.reasoning: A container-tooling engineer can repair the four stages in a few hours; the corrected logic is a few hundred lines of Go, far under 100k.

rubric_axes.name: Difficult
rubric_axes.reasoning: It requires real overlay and OCI image-format expertise plus multi-stage debugging under coupling; it is not solvable by a trick, a magic flag, or an obscure fact.

rubric_axes.name: Interesting
rubric_axes.reasoning: Userspace image-layer flattening that matches kernel overlay semantics is real container-tooling work that maintainers of umoci and containers/storage are paid to get right.

rubric_axes.name: Outcome-verified
rubric_axes.reasoning: Grading walks the produced tree and checks overlay-equivalent outcomes; it does not dictate the algorithm and accepts any implementation that flattens correctly.

instruction_completeness_test.reasoning: Reading only instruction.md, an agent knows the run interface, the input layer format, the output expectation (match a kernel overlay mount), and the preservation constraints, but not which pipeline stage is wrong or how the four merge concerns are mishandled; the code, runtime behaviour, and docs are essential, so the instruction is not a recipe.

discovery_budget.discovery: An opaque directory marker hides only the contributions of strictly-lower layers; entries the marking layer itself provides and entries added by higher layers in that directory must remain.
discovery_budget.planned_location: behaviour of the opaque stage plus the worked rules in environment/docs/overlay-semantics.md
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Stating that the opaque pass is sticky and over-masks would localize the defect to one stage and turn the diagnosis into transcription.

discovery_budget.discovery: A whiteout of a directory removes the entire lower subtree, not just the exact path, and a whiteout only affects layers below its origin, so a path re-created in a higher layer reappears.
discovery_budget.planned_location: behaviour of the whiteout stage plus environment/docs/overlay-semantics.md
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Revealing that descendants are orphaned or that precedence is layer-insensitive would name the cause and the fix site.

discovery_budget.discovery: When a path is present in more than one layer, its merged metadata (permission bits and ownership) is taken from the topmost contributing layer, and this holds for directories as well as files.
discovery_budget.planned_location: behaviour of the attribute-resolution stage plus environment/docs/overlay-semantics.md
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Saying copy-up currently takes the wrong layer would hand the solver the inverted-direction bug directly.

discovery_budget.discovery: Tar hardlink groups within a layer must be materialized as shared inodes in the output, and group membership must exclude members that an upper layer removed or replaced.
discovery_budget.planned_location: behaviour of the materialization stage plus environment/docs/overlay-semantics.md
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Disclosing that the emitter dereferences groups or links to stale members would point at the final stage and its coupling to whiteouts.

instruction_specificity.reasoning: Repair shape: the instruction describes the observable divergences from a kernel overlay mount (entries that should be hidden remain, entries that should remain are missing, some paths carry the wrong ownership or permissions, and some files that share storage are emitted as independent copies) and points at the public overlay contract and docs, while withholding the cause, the diverging stages, and the merge algorithm.

attack_path.summary: Read the overlay-semantics doc and the pipeline, flatten the bundled stacks and diff against expected overlay behaviour, then localize and repair the four coordinated stages so opaque scope, whiteout subtree and precedence, copy-up direction, and link grouping all match.
attack_path.reasoning: Each symptom class points at a different stage but only after the solver reconstructs the overlay contract: surviving entries are decided in the union stages, the metadata winner in attribute resolution, and shared inodes in materialization. Because the parity tests exercise every concern at once, the solver must coordinate all four repairs and confirm the fix generalizes to verify-time stacks rather than the bundled fixtures.

smallest_plausible_patch.summary: Correct the four stage helpers so the flattened tree matches overlay semantics across opaque, whiteout, copy-up, and hardlink interactions on bundled and generated stacks.
smallest_plausible_patch.reasoning: There is no smaller patch: each defect controls a disjoint symptom family, so leaving any one wrong fails its dedicated tests and both parity tests; the genuine change is several dozen lines of coordinated merge logic spread across four packages, well above a trivial revert.

collapse_audit.reasoning: After full disclosure of the overlay contract the residual work is diagnosing and repairing four coupled stages of a working pipeline, validated on verify-time-generated stacks by an output-tree walk; no surface signal on instruction, file names, or tests shortcuts the diagnosis.
collapse_audit.residual_hardness: Coordinating opaque masking scope, directory-whiteout subtree removal and layer-confined precedence, topmost metadata copy-up, and hardlink-group preservation across a four-stage pipeline so the flattened tree equals a kernel overlay mount on unseen layer stacks.

topology_enumeration.summary: Repair surviving-entry decisions: opaque scope and whiteout subtree removal feeding correct emission.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Opaque scope in sift and subtree removal in cull jointly determine which entries survive, and braid must emit exactly that set; fixing only one leaves either masked-but-present or orphaned entries that the parity tests catch.

topology_enumeration.summary: Repair metadata copy-up consistently with re-creation across layers and the shared index types.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Copy-up in vouch must read the topmost contributor recorded in the index types, and re-creation handled in cull changes which layer is topmost; a copy-up fix that ignores re-creation picks a stale layer and fails the directory-metadata cases.

topology_enumeration.summary: Repair link-group preservation coupled to whiteouts and emission.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Materialization in braid must produce shared inodes for the link group recorded in the index types, but cull decides which members an upper layer removed; emitting the recorded group without honouring removals links to a phantom entry and fails the link tests.

symbol_table.purpose: restricts a directory marker so it suppresses only contributions from levels below the one that declared it

symbol_table.signature: func reap(a *model.Index, lv int, p string)
symbol_table.purpose: removes a path and its whole descendant range from the accumulated set when a lower level is suppressed at the given level
selects the attribute record contributed by the highest level that provides the node
materializes a storage-sharing set as one backing object plus additional references for the surviving members

decoy_manifest.non_fix_purpose: aggregates per-level entry counters for the run report only and never changes the accumulated set
renders an ASCII view of the accumulated index for dry-run console output
formats diagnostic log lines for the suite runner off the materialization path

renames_during_drafting.reason: a function named after the opaque marker would telegraph the masking-scope defect; renamed to an opaque helper in a neutral package.
a name built from the whiteout noun would grep straight to the subtree-removal fix; renamed to a neutral helper.
a copy-up name encodes the metadata-resolution concept the instruction discusses; renamed to a neutral helper.
a name containing link would collide with an instruction noun and reveal the hardlink stage; renamed to a neutral helper.

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: simple single-layer or non-conflicting stacks flatten correctly, so the tool looks finished while the defects only surface on opaque, multi-layer, or linked stacks
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: a solver that validates on an easy stack sees a passing run and stops before exercising the multi-component interactions that fail
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: the overlay contract and the run interface are fully documented so the solver can construct the revealing stacks itself

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: the bottom-up accumulation carries opaque scope, surviving entries, the topmost contributor, and link-group membership that later stages consume
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: repairing one stage in isolation corrupts the state a downstream stage depends on, for example fixing subtree removal without updating link-group membership, which is a multi-component state-reconstruction failure
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: the accumulated index types and the per-stage responsibilities are visible in the codebase and docs

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: each buggy stage emits locally plausible output: an opaque directory looks masked, attributes look set, and files look written, so per-stage inspection reads as correct
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: a locally plausible stage passes a spot check yet violates overlay equivalence only when composed with the other stages on a tricky stack
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: the expected composed behaviour is stated as overlay-mount equivalence, which the solver can check end to end

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: the tar layer format, the in-memory accumulated index, the materialized output tree, and the run report must all agree on the same overlay semantics
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: a partial implementation that handles one representation emits a report that round-trips but fails an output-tree walk under the independent verifier
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: every input format detail and output expectation is documented in the instruction and environment docs

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: the OCI whiteout conventions (a per-name prefix marker and a directory opaque marker) and copy-up precedence are domain-specific conventions the solver must apply precisely
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: a solver unfamiliar with the exact conventions mishandles the opaque marker scope or the directory-versus-file whiteout distinction
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: the conventions are named and defined in environment/docs/overlay-semantics.md so they are discoverable rather than secret

calibration_plan.human_sanity: a container-tooling engineer can solve by reading the overlay-semantics doc, flattening the bundled stacks, diffing against expected overlay behaviour, and repairing opaque scope, whiteout subtree and precedence, topmost copy-up, and link-group preservation.
calibration_plan.shortcut_audit: tests rebuild and rerun the solver, generate fresh stacks at verify time, reject static output trees, walk the produced tree rather than trusting the run report, and reject a solver that special-cases the bundled fixtures.
calibration_plan.ablation_plan: revert each of the four stage helpers independently and confirm only that stage's dedicated tests plus the two parity tests fail, while the other stages' tests still pass.

pass_rate_target.basis: stronger target agent; aligns with Part E Hard/Medium/Easy thresholds on worst/best model accuracy. The verifier is offline (pytest baked into the Dockerfile, no runtime installs under allow_internet = false) and post-upload difficulty is classified by Part E after platform agent runs.

metrics.criterion: presence and absence of entries, ownership, and permissions in the flattened tree match a kernel overlay mount on bundled and generated stacks

opaque masking is confined to strictly-lower layers, a directory whiteout removes the whole lower subtree, copy-up takes the topmost contributor, and storage-sharing sets become shared inodes minus removed members
re-flattening the same stack yields an identical deterministic tree digest and the suite leaves the binary and report in place
the flatten command and the shell runners read the documented inputs and write the documented output tree and report shape
every bundled stack appears in the report with its observation fields and the output tree is fully materialized

verifier_scoring_plan.binary_threshold_rule: reward is 1 only on pytest exit 0; otherwise reward is 0

subtype_milestone_plan.sidecar_or_protocol_notes: run-suite.sh flattens the bundled stacks and writes one JSON report; verifier tests invoke the flatten binary on temporary generated stacks

platform_files.role: Edition 2 metadata and runtime limits; sets [environment] allow_internet = false
symptoms-only public contract for the run interface, input layer format, and overlay-mount equivalence
local output declaration for the generated run report
verifier entrypoint; invokes pre-installed pytest only
independent verifier that builds layer stacks with known ground truth and walks the flattened output tree
oracle that repairs the four stage helpers and rebuilds
digest-pinned offline Go image with pytest, tmux, and asciinema
authoring metadata for fix frontier, base image, decoys, and the flipping-point contract

task_files.role: entrypoint that reads ordered layer archives and writes the flattened tree
shared accumulated-index, node, group, and stat types
readable detection of the per-name and opaque whiteout conventions (active decoy, not on the fix path)
materialization stage with storage-sharing handling (defect shipped)
worked documentation of the overlay union and whiteout conventions
bundled ordered layer archives that exercise every concern
flattens the bundled stacks and writes the report

fix_frontier.distribution: four pipeline stages across four neutral packages: opaque scope, per-name whiteout precedence and subtree removal, topmost attribute copy-up, and storage-sharing materialization.
fix_frontier.naming_policy: neutral internal package directories each exposing a stable entry wrapper, with the defect localized to one opaquely named unexported helper per package; shared domain types live in a non-fix package and the whiteout-convention detector is a non-fix decoy.
fix_frontier.helpers_policy: sibling helper packages render diagnostics, aggregate counters, and format log lines that rhyme with the stage helpers but never change the accumulated set or the output tree.

preferred_assertion_styles: walk the flattened output tree and check entry presence and absence
stat output paths and compare permission and ownership bits to the topmost contributor
compare inode numbers and link counts to confirm shared storage
rebuild and rerun the solver on freshly generated stacks
forbidden_assertion_styles: stack to path to expected boolean tables

task_shape.collapse_risk: medium; mitigated by verify-time-generated stacks that defeat hardcoding, an output-tree walk that ignores the tamperable run report, a non-textbook overlay-semantics fix, and a fix frontier split across four coupled stages.

category_profile.challenge_family: union-merge reconstruction of a flattened filesystem from stacked OCI layers under overlay semantics
category_profile.allowed_instruction_disclosures: the run interface and commands, the ordered-layer input format, the OCI whiteout conventions, the requirement to match a kernel overlay mount, the preservation constraints for ownership/permissions and shared storage, and the deterministic re-run expectation.
category_profile.forbidden_instruction_leaks: the diverging stage identifiers, the cause of each divergence, the merge algorithm, per-stack expected values, and any statement of which stage controls which test.
category_profile.category_specific_hardness_bar: the canonical flattened state must be inferred from conflicting contributions across multiple layers (opaque scope, whiteout precedence, topmost copy-up, shared storage), and no single stage's output is the canonical answer because the concerns are decided in different stages that consume each other's state.
category_profile.category_specific_verifier_risks: leaking a golden flattened tree, trusting the tamperable run report instead of the output tree, coupling tests to directory iteration order, or a hidden-instance puzzle over one bundled stack.
category_profile.coverage_role: adds the first filesystem_state_reconstruction profile, the first repair_existing_system shape, and the first software-engineering category task to a bank that otherwise holds a Go optimization task and a Rust reverse-engineering task.

satisfiability_risk.rc2_planned_name_risk: low: the four stage helpers carry neutral opaque names in neutral packages and the only domain-named package is the non-fix marker detector decoy.
satisfiability_risk.gx9_contract_risk: low: the instruction states the overlay contract and preservation rules but no per-stack expected values; expectations are recomputed from the constructed stacks.
satisfiability_risk.cr1_symbol_frontier_risk: low: the manifest fixes the four helper symbols and the oracle edits them verbatim within their own packages.
satisfiability_risk.hidden_contract_risk: low: every tested behaviour (presence, ownership, permissions, shared inodes, determinism) is a consequence of the overlay contract stated in the instruction and docs.

actionability_plan.verifier_command_visible: instruction.md names the flatten command, bash /app/scripts/flatten-one.sh and bash /app/scripts/run-suite.sh, and the pytest verifier command.
actionability_plan.source_fix_intent_visible: instruction.md says the solver sources live under /app/environment and that writing a static output tree is insufficient because the verifier re-runs the solver on new stacks.
actionability_plan.generated_output_rule_visible: instruction.md identifies /app/output/flatten-report.json and the per-call output tree and states that stacks not under /app/environment/testdata are also flattened.
actionability_plan.exact_formula_home: instruction.md and /app/environment/docs/overlay-semantics.md state the whiteout conventions, opaque scope, copy-up precedence, and shared-storage preservation rule.
actionability_plan.schema_home: instruction.md names the input layer format and the report fields; the overlay-semantics doc carries the worked union rules.

waiver_plan.waiver_rationale: no waiver is planned; any checker warning should be resolved or documented before packaging.

reference_pattern.justification_if_none: Original task built from real Linux overlayfs union semantics and the OCI image-spec whiteout conventions (per-name .wh. prefix and the .wh..wh..opq opaque marker) as implemented by userspace flatteners such as umoci and containers/storage; not derived from a website inspiration, a prior public task, or a template reskin. The reference library currently has no promoted entries.

realism_source.evidence_basis: Linux overlayfs union and copy-up semantics and the OCI image-spec whiteout conventions (the per-name .wh. prefix whiteout and the .wh..wh..opq opaque-directory marker), as implemented by userspace image flatteners such as umoci and containers/storage.
realism_source.upstream_or_synthetic_rationale: the overlay union rules, whiteout conventions, copy-up precedence, and hardlink preservation are taken from the kernel overlayfs documentation and the OCI image-spec; the specific layer stacks are synthetic to keep all data local and deterministic.
realism_source.minimization_preserves: opaque masking confined to strictly-lower layers, directory-whiteout subtree removal and layer-confined precedence, topmost-contributor metadata copy-up including for directories, and tar hardlink-group preservation as shared inodes.
realism_source.synthetic_exception_review: not a synthetic exception; the semantics are drawn from real subsystems and a real image-format spec, and only the layer stacks and thresholds are generated.
